name: DRAM wafer capacity (HBM-eligible nodes)
severityNote: HBM is consuming the marginal wafer; commodity tightening
description: Total wafer-equivalent capacity at the three DRAM makers (SK Hynix, Samsung, Micron) sets the upstream ceiling on every HBM stack. Because HBM consumes roughly 3× the wafer area per equivalent bit of DDR5, allocation to HBM directly pulls wafer-starts away from commodity DRAM and tightens the broader memory market.
stackPosition: Silicon wafer (Shin-Etsu, SUMCO) → DRAM fab front-end (1c/1d-nm nodes for HBM3E/HBM4 base die) → TSV etch + plating → singulation → stacking (TCB or hybrid bonding) → HBM cube → CoWoS attach. The wafer-fab stage is the upstream constraint; total wafer-area allocated to HBM directly subtracts from DDR5 / LPDDR5 / GDDR7 / NAND capacity at the same fab.

supplyStructure.name: CXMT (China-domestic)
supplyStructure.note: Export-controlled out of frontier AI memory

indicators.name: SK Hynix wafer-start capacity guidance and HBM allocation mix
Samsung memory segment wafer-start commentary and P4 ramp
Micron HBM mix and total fab capacity guidance
TrendForce + DRAMeXchange contract pricing for DDR5 / LPDDR5
CXMT capacity expansion announcements and export-control delta

companies.name: SK Hynix
companies.note: Most HBM-skewed wafer mix; M15X is the dedicated HBM fab

companies.name: Samsung Electronics
companies.note: Largest absolute wafer capacity; HBM4 qualification is the swing factor

companies.name: Micron Technology
companies.note: US-listed pure-play; HBM mix climbing aggressively

companies.name: Hanmi Semiconductor
companies.note: TC-bonder market share = wafer capacity sensitivity proxy

companies.name: BE Semiconductor
companies.note: Hybrid bonding extends the wafer-cycle's investable surface

insight: DRAM wafer capacity is the cleanest 'shovels' play in the AI memory thesis. Industry wafer-start growth at 8-10% YoY cannot keep pace with HBM demand growth at 50%+, which means commodity DRAM allocation gets squeezed even as HBM eats the marginal wafer. The cleanest exposures are Hynix + Micron with Samsung as the call option on HBM4 qualification. The under-priced wedge is HBM's second-order tightening of LPDDR5 supply for mobile and DDR5 for general server — a re-rating that affects every memory-bill-of-materials in tech.

debates.question: Does HBM wafer-area mix exceed 30% of total industry DRAM by end of 2027?
debates.currentBelief: Likely on current capex trajectories; consensus closer to 27-28%

debates.question: Does commodity DRAM contract pricing inflect upward through 2H 2026 as HBM consumes wafer-starts?
debates.currentBelief: Yes — already showing in TrendForce monthly index
